identity.short_name: Minimum devices (odd a,b) to sum to 12 with terms a/b+b/a

subdomain(s): Diophantine representation with coprime and parity constraints
topic_path: minimal number of terms in constrained rational sums

problem_spec.summary: Each device contributes a rational term a/b + b/a with a,b positive odd integers. Determine the minimum number of terms whose sum is a given integer using coprime normalization, mod-3 obstruction for two terms via denominator alignment, and a mod-4 parity constraint from odd denominators; then give a construction at the minimal k.
givens: One device has resistance R = a/b + b/a where a,b are positive odd integers.
ask: Find the smallest number k of devices needed so the total resistance equals 12.

parameter(s).name: term_form
parameter(s).domain: a/b + b/a with a,b in Z_{>0}

parameter(s).name: parity_constraint
parameter(s).purpose: restrict each pair (a,b) to be odd, enabling mod-4 residue control

invariant(s).name: coprime_reduction
invariant(s).statement: Replacing (a,b) by (a/g,b/g) with g=gcd(a,b) leaves a/b+b/a unchanged, so one may assume gcd(a,b)=1 for each term; oddness is preserved under this reduction.

invariant(s).name: single_term_quadratic
invariant(s).statement: If T = a/b + b/a then (a/b) satisfies t^2 - T t + 1 = 0, so integer T occurs for one term only when T^2-4 is a perfect square (hence only T=2).

invariant(s).name: two_term_alignment
invariant(s).statement: If (a^2+b^2)/(ab) + (c^2+d^2)/(cd) is an integer and gcd(a,b)=gcd(c,d)=1, then ab=cd (since ab|cd and cd|ab by reducing modulo each denominator using gcd_lemma).

invariant(s).name: mod3_residue_of_sum_of_squares
invariant(s).statement: For coprime integers a,b, a^2+b^2 \equiv 1 (mod3) iff exactly one of a,b is divisible by 3, and \equiv 2 (mod3) iff neither is divisible by 3; it is never 0 (mod3).

invariant(s).name: mod4_residue_for_odd_pairs
invariant(s).statement: If a,b are odd and gcd(a,b)=1, then (a^2+b^2)/(ab) has a well-defined residue mod 4 (denominator odd) and is always \equiv2 (mod4).

essential_assumption(s): a,b are positive odd integers; different devices may use different (a,b).
Series connection corresponds to sum of resistances.
Congruence manipulations are applied after ensuring denominators are invertible mod the chosen modulus (e.g., mod 4 for odd denominators).
uniqueness_certificate(s): A construction with k terms gives an upper bound; congruence/gcd obstructions for fewer terms (including a parity constraint forcing k even) give a matching lower bound, pinning down the unique minimum k.

technique(s).name: coprime_normalization
technique(s).purpose: reduce to gcd(a,b)=1 without changing the term, preserving oddness
outputs: normalized coprime pairs enabling gcd and congruence arguments

technique(s).name: prove_one_term_impossible
technique(s).purpose: rule out k=1 via the quadratic/discriminant criterion

technique(s).name: prove_two_terms_impossible
inputs: equality of two terms summing to T and gcd_lemma
technique(s).purpose: use two_term_alignment (ab=cd) and mod-3 residues to contradict when T is divisible by 3

technique(s).name: parity_obstruction_from_odd_pairs
inputs: oddness constraint on every (a,b) and target T
technique(s).purpose: show each term is \equiv2 (mod4), hence the total sum forces k to be even; eliminate k=3
outputs: k must be even, so k\neq3

technique(s).name: give_four_term_construction
technique(s).purpose: produce an explicit representation of T with k=4

technique(s).name: combine_bounds
inputs: lower bounds from impossibility arguments and an explicit construction

theorem(s).name: gcd properties for coprime integers
theorem(s).role: justify gcd(a^2+b^2,ab)=1 and enable denominator divisibility arguments

theorem(s).name: modular arithmetic of squares mod 3
theorem(s).role: characterize a^2+b^2 residues mod 3 for coprime pairs

theorem(s).name: invertibility of odd denominators mod 4
theorem(s).role: justify taking residues of (even)/(odd) rationals modulo 4

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Congruences and coprime constraints control Diophantine representations; parity constraints add a mod-4 restriction on the number of terms.

critical_trick(s): Normalize to coprime pairs and use gcd(a^2+b^2,ab)=1.
For two terms, reduce modulo each denominator to force ab=cd, then use mod-3 residues of sums of squares to contradict when T is divisible by 3.
Use the oddness constraint to show each term is \equiv2 (mod4), forcing k to be even for targets \equiv0 (mod4).
Provide an explicit 4-term construction achieving T exactly.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: brute-force search over small odd (a,b) pairs to find constructions and test minimality candidates

library(s).name: fractions
library(s).reason: exact rational arithmetic (Fraction) when verifying sums

task(s).name: brute_force_min_k_search
task(s).reason: enumerate odd coprime pairs (a,b) up to a bound and check if T can be formed with k=2,3,4 terms to corroborate the theoretical minimal k

task(s).name: verify_congruence_obstructions
task(s).reason: experimentally confirm no k=2 solutions exist and no k=3 solutions exist (consistent with mod-4 parity) within a large search bound
